translation_text: I will keep My covenant with you, that never again will all flesh be cut off by flood waters, never again will flood waters destroy the earth.

translation_text: Elohim said, This is the sign of the covenant that I pledge between Myself and you, and between every living creature that is with you, for the generations of all time.

translation_text: Come, let us descend and jumble their language, that they will not understand one another’s language.

translation_text: This is the history of Yaakov; Yoseif at the age of seventeen years, would tend the sheep with his brothers, and the lad was with the sons of Bilhah, and the sons of Zilpah, his father’s wives. Yoseif brought back bad reports about them to their father.

translation_text: His brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, and they hated him. They could not speak to him peaceably.

translation_text: Yoseif had a dream and he told his brothers, and they hated him even more.

translation_text: He said to them, Listen to this dream that I dreamt.

translation_text: Behold, we were binding sheaves in the middle of the field. Behold my sheaf rose and stood up straight; and behold your sheaves surrounded it and prostrated themselves to my sheaf.

translation_text: His brothers said to him, Will you then be a king over us? Will you indeed rule over us? They hated him even more because of his dreams and his words.

translation_text: He had another dream and told it to his brothers. He said, Behold! I dreamed another dream. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars were prostrating themselves to me.

translation_text: He told it to his father and to his brothers. His father rebuked him, and said to him, What is this dream that you dreamed? Shall I, your mother and your brothers come and prostrate themselves on the ground to you?

translation_text: We said, We cannot go down. If our youngest brother is with us, we will go down, for we cannot see the man’s face, unless our youngest brother is with us.

translation_text: Your servant, my father said to us, You know that my wife [Rochel] bore me two sons.

translation_text: One has [already] left me, and I said, surely he is torn to pieces. I have not seen him until now.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: If you take this one also away from me, and misfortune befall him, you will bring my white head down to the grave in evil.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: And now, when I come to your servant, my father, and the lad is not with us; his soul is bound up with the lad’s soul.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: When he sees that the lad is not [with us], he will die. Your servants will have brought down the white head of your servant, our father, to the grave in sorrow.

translation_text: For your servant became surety for the lad, to my father, saying, If I do not bring him to you, I will have sinned to my father for all time.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: And now, let your servant remain as a slave to my master instead of the lad. Let the lad go up with his brothers.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: For how shall I go up to my father when the lad is not with me; lest I see the evil that would befall my father.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif could not contain his emotions in the presence of all who stood before him, and he cried out Let everyone leave my presence. No man remained with him, when Yoseif made himself known to his brothers.

translation_text: He wept aloud, and the Egyptians heard about it, and the house of Pharaoh [also] heard.

translation_text: Yoseif said to his brothers, I am Yoseif, is my father still alive? His brothers could not answer him for they were shocked at his presence.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: [Then] Yoseif said to his brothers, Please come close to me. They came close [to him] and he said, I am Yoseif your brother, whom you sold into Egypt.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Now do not worry, and do not be angry with yourselves that you sold me here; for it was to preserve life that Elohim sent me [here] before you.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: For it is [now] two years that there has been famine in the land; and for another five years there will be no plowing or harvest.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Elohim sent me [here] before you to insure your survival in the land, and to keep you alive for a great deliverance.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Now [then] it was not you that sent me here, but Elohim; and He has made me as a father to Pharaoh, and master of all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Hurry, go up to my father and tell him: this is what your son, Yoseif, says, Elohim has made me master of all Egypt. Come down to me, do not delay.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: You will dwell in the land of Goshen, and you will be close to me—you, your children, your grandchildren, your sheep, your cattle and all that you own.

translation_text: I will provide for you there, since there will be another five years of famine; lest you become impoverished together with your household and all that is yours.

translation_text: Behold, your eyes see it along with my brother Binyomin’s eyes, that I speak to you with my own mouth.

translation_text: Tell my father of all my honor in Egypt, and all that you saw. Hurry and bring my father down here.

translation_text: He [then] fell upon his brother Binyomin’s neck and wept, and Binyomin wept upon his neck.

translation_text: The news was heard in Pharaoh’s house that Yoseif’s brothers had come. This was good [news] in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants.

translation_text: Yoseif harnessed his chariot, and went up to greet his father, Yisrael in Goshen. [When] he appeared before him, he fell on his neck, and wept on his neck for a long time.

translation_text: Yoseif said to his brothers and to his father’s household, I will go up and tell Pharaoh. I will say to him, My brothers and my father’s household, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.

license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: And when Pharaoh calls you, and says, What is your occupation,
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: You should say, Your servants have been livestock owners from our youth until now, we and our fathers; so that you will be able to settle in the land of Goshen, since every shepherd is abhorrent to Egypt.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif came and told Pharaoh, and he said, My father, and my brothers, their sheep, their cattle, and all their possessions, have come from the land of Canaan, and they are now in the land of Goshen.

translation_text: From among his brothers, he took five men, and he presented them to Pharaoh.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Pharaoh said to his brothers, What is your occupation? They said to Pharaoh, Your servants are shepherds, we and our fathers.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: They said to Pharaoh, We have come to live in the land temporarily, since there is no pasture for your servant’s flocks, because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now then, please let your servants settle in the land of Goshen.

translation_text: Pharaoh said to Yoseif, Your father and your brothers have come to you.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: The land of Egypt is before you. In the best of the land, settle your father and your brothers. Let them settle in the land of Goshen. If you know of capable men among them, appoint them livestock officers over my [herds.]
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif [then] brought in Yaakov, his father, and presented him to Pharaoh; and Yaakov blessed Pharaoh.

translation_text: Yaakov said to Pharaoh, The years of my temporary residence are one hundred and thirty years. Few and troublesome have been the days of my life. I have not attained the years of my father’s lives, in the days of their temporary residence.

translation_text: Yaakov blessed Pharaoh, and left Pharaoh’s presence.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif settled his father and his brothers, and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best part of the land, in the land of Ramseis, as Pharaoh had ordered.

translation_text: Yoseif provided his father, his brothers, and all his father’s household with bread according to [the needs of] the children.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: There was no bread in all the land, for the famine was very severe. The land of Egypt, and Canaan were worn out because of the famine.

translation_text: Yoseif gathered up all the money that was to be found in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, through the purchases they made. Yoseif brought the money to the house of Pharaoh.

translation_text: When the money was used up in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, all [the people of] Egypt came to Yoseif, saying, Give us bread; why should we die in your presence, [just] because there is no money?
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif said, Bring your livestock, and I will give you [food] [in exchange] for your livestock if there is no more money.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: They brought their livestock to Yoseif, and Yoseif gave them bread [in exchange] for their horses, their flocks of sheep, their herds of cattle and their donkeys. He tended them with bread in exchange for all their livestock, in that year.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: That year came to an end. They came to him in the second year, and they said to him, We are holding nothing back from my master, for the money is used up, and the herds of cattle belong to my master. There is nothing left before my master except our bodies and our land.

translation_text: Why should we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land [in exchange] for bread. We and our land will become slaves to Pharaoh. Give us seed grain, let us live and not die, and let the land not become desolate.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, for every Egyptian sold his field, because the famine was severe upon them. Thus the land became Pharaoh’s [property.]
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: He [Yoseif] then transferred the people to cities, from one end of the border of Egypt to the other.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: But the land of the priests he did not buy, because the priests had an allotment from Pharaoh. They ate the allotment that Pharaoh gave them, and therefore they did not sell their land.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif said to the people, Behold, I have today purchased you and your lands for Pharaoh. Here is seed, plant [it in] the soil.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: When it produces you must give a fifth to Pharaoh. Four parts shall be yours for seed for your fields and for your food, and for those of your households, and for food for your little ones.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: They said, You have saved our lives. May we find favor in the eyes of my master, and we will be slaves to Pharaoh.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yoseif enacted a statute over the land of Egypt to this day, that a fifth belongs to Pharaoh. Except the land of the priests alone, did not belong to Pharaoh.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yisrael lived in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen. They acquired property there. They were fruitful and [their population] increased greatly.
license_note: Sefaria returned license=CC-BY. Full text was persisted for source-pipeline work, but future production use still requires human review and attribution checks.

translation_text: Yaakov lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years. The days of Yaakov, the years of his life were one hundred and forty seven years.

translation_text: The days of Yisrael’s death drew near, and he called for his son Yoseif, and said to him, If I have found favor in your eyes, please, place your hand under my thigh; that you will deal kindly and truthfully with me. Please, do not bury me in Egypt.

translation_text: This is what you should say to Yoseif, Please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin, for they did evil to you. And now please forgive the transgr ession of the servants of the God of your father. Yoseif wept as his brothers spoke to him.
